title: Today's AI Brief: The Token Bill Comes Due
summary: The AI industry is facing a reality check on costs, with companies struggling to manage the expenses of AI development and deployment.
imageAlt: Image from source article for The token bill comes due: Inside the industry scramble to manage AI’s runaway costs
imageCaption: The token bill comes due: Inside the industry scramble to manage AI’s runaway costs
imageSourceTitle: The token bill comes due: Inside the industry scramble to manage AI’s runaway costs

body.text: I'm looking at some interesting developments in the world of AI, and it seems the industry is facing a bit of a wake-up call. Companies are starting to feel the pinch of AI's runaway costs. According to a report from TechCrunch, Uber blew through its entire 2026 AI coding budget by April. That's like burning through your entire year's coffee budget in the first month - not exactly the most sustainable approach. This has led to some interesting moves, like Microsoft revoking its developers' Claude Code licenses months after enabling them. It's clear that the costs associated with AI development and deployment are becoming unsustainable for some companies.

The scramble to manage AI costs is on, and it's not just about cutting back on licenses or budgets. Some companies are exploring new ways to optimize their AI deployments, while others are looking for more cost-effective solutions. It's a bit like trying to find the best deals on Black Friday - except instead of scoring discounts on gadgets, you're trying to save on compute power.
Trump's AI Order and Smart Glasses

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
